title: Classic Greek Salad (Horiatiki)
subtitle: Few, top-quality ingredients — just like in Greece
image_prompt: Authentic Greek village salad (Horiatiki) in a rustic bowl: chunky ripe tomatoes, thick cucumber slices, green pepper rings, red onion, kalamata olives, topped with a whole block of feta drizzled with olive oil and dried oregano, bright Mediterranean daylight, overhead editorial food photography

steps.title: Chop the vegetables
steps.text: Roughly chop the tomatoes, cut the cucumber into thick slices, slice the green pepper into rings and the red onion into thin rings. Add everything to a large bowl.
steps.tip: Tomatoes must be at room temperature — never straight from the fridge. It makes a huge difference in flavour.

steps.title: Salt, rest & add olives
steps.text: Season lightly with sea salt and let it sit for 5 minutes, then scatter over the kalamata olives.

steps.title: Top with feta
steps.text: Lay the feta on top as one large block — don't crumble it.

steps.title: Dress & serve
steps.text: Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil, sprinkle oregano over the feta and salad, add an optional splash of red wine vinegar, and serve immediately.
steps.tip: A real Horiatiki has no lettuce and no dressing other than good olive oil, oregano and salt.

title: Spanish Tortilla with Herbed Yogurt & Greens
subtitle: The classic, lighter and fresher with Mediterranean toppings
image_prompt: Modern Spanish tortilla wedge on a plate, golden and slightly juicy in the centre, topped with a dollop of herbed Greek yogurt, crumbled feta, sumac, chilli flakes and fresh microgreens, bright Mediterranean daylight, overhead editorial food photography

steps.title: Cook the potatoes & onion
steps.text: Peel and thinly slice the potatoes and the sweet onion. Warm the olive oil in a pan and cook them gently over medium-low heat for about 20 minutes until soft but not browned. Drain, keeping a little oil.
steps.tip: Low and slow, confit-style — this keeps the potatoes silky rather than fried.

steps.title: Mix with the eggs
steps.text: Beat the eggs with a good pinch of sea salt. Fold in the warm potatoes and onion and let it sit for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Set the tortilla
steps.text: Heat a little oil in a non-stick pan, pour in the mixture and cook over medium-low heat for 4–5 minutes until the edges set. Flip with a plate and cook another 3–4 minutes, keeping the centre slightly soft.
steps.tip: A juicy, slightly soft centre is the sign of a proper tortilla.

steps.title: Make the herbed yogurt
steps.text: Stir the Greek yogurt with the lemon juice, grated garlic and the chopped dill, parsley and mint. Season to taste.

steps.title: Serve with fresh toppings
steps.text: Slice the tortilla, spoon the herbed yogurt on top, crumble over the feta, dust with sumac and chilli flakes and finish with microgreens or rocket.

title: Frozen Greek Yogurt Berry Bark
subtitle: A crunchy frozen treat that tastes like dessert, not diet food
image_prompt: Frozen Greek yogurt bark broken into shards on a marble board, topped with raspberries, blueberries and dark chocolate shavings, bright airy food photography

steps.title: Sweeten the yogurt
steps.text: Stir the Greek yogurt with the honey and a little vanilla until smooth and creamy.

steps.title: Spread & top
steps.text: Spread the yogurt onto a lined tray in a ~1 cm layer. Scatter the berries and chopped dark chocolate over the top.
steps.tip: Press the berries in lightly so they freeze into the bark.

steps.title: Freeze & break
steps.text: Freeze for at least 3 hours until solid, then break into shards. Keep in the freezer until serving.

title: Chocolate-Peanut Banana Nice Cream
subtitle: Two-minute soft-serve from frozen bananas — no ice-cream machine
image_prompt: Creamy chocolate banana nice cream swirled in a bowl, dusted with cocoa and a swirl of peanut butter, soft natural light

steps.title: Freeze ahead
steps.text: Slice ripe bananas and freeze for at least 3 hours (best done the night before).
steps.tip: The riper the bananas, the sweeter the nice cream — no added sugar needed.

steps.title: Blend smooth
steps.text: Blend the frozen banana with peanut butter, cocoa, plant milk and a pinch of salt until thick and creamy like soft-serve.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Eat straight away as soft-serve, or freeze for 1 hour for a scoopable texture.

title: Berry Chia Pudding Parfait
subtitle: Make-ahead pudding that sets overnight — creamy, fruity, no cooking
image_prompt: Chia pudding parfait layered with fresh berries in a glass, topped with mint, bright clean food photography

steps.title: Mix the pudding
steps.text: Whisk the chia seeds with the milk, maple syrup and a little vanilla. Let it rest 5 minutes, then whisk again to break up any clumps.
steps.tip: The second whisk is the trick to a smooth, lump-free pudding.

steps.title: Chill to set
steps.text: Cover and chill for at least 4 hours, or overnight, until thick and spoonable.

steps.title: Layer & serve
steps.text: Layer the chia pudding with the berries in glasses and serve cold.

title: No-Bake Chocolate Peanut Oat Bars
subtitle: Chewy oat bars with a dark-chocolate top — no oven needed
image_prompt: No-bake oat bars with a glossy dark chocolate top, cut into squares on parchment, cozy food photography

steps.title: Mix the base
steps.text: Gently warm the peanut butter with the maple syrup until runny, then stir into the oats with a pinch of salt until evenly coated.

steps.title: Press & chill
steps.text: Press the mixture firmly into a lined tin and chill for 30 minutes until set.
steps.tip: Press hard — well-compacted bars hold together when you cut them.

steps.title: Chocolate top
steps.text: Melt the dark chocolate, spread over the top, chill until set, then cut into bars.

title: Banana Oat Chocolate-Chip Cookies
subtitle: Soft cookies sweetened mostly by ripe banana
image_prompt: Soft banana oat cookies with melting dark chocolate chips on a cooling rack, warm homely light

steps.title: Mash & mix
steps.text: Mash the bananas well, then stir in the oats, peanut butter, a little cinnamon and the chocolate chips into a thick dough.

steps.title: Shape
steps.text: Spoon mounds onto a lined tray and flatten gently into cookies — they don't spread much.

steps.title: Bake
steps.text: Bake at 180°C for 12–15 minutes until golden at the edges. Cool on a rack to firm up.

title: Baked Cinnamon Apples with Oat Crumble
subtitle: All the apple-crumble cosiness, in single portions
image_prompt: Baked whole apples stuffed with golden oat-walnut crumble in a rustic dish, steam rising, warm cosy light

steps.title: Prep the apples
steps.text: Core the apples and set them upright in a baking dish.

steps.title: Make the crumble
steps.text: Mix the oats, chopped walnuts, soft butter, maple syrup and plenty of cinnamon into a crumble, then spoon it into and over the apples.

steps.title: Bake
steps.text: Bake at 180°C for 25–30 minutes until the apples are soft and the topping is golden. Serve warm.
steps.tip: Lovely with a spoon of Greek yogurt on top.

title: Peanut-Butter Stuffed Dates
subtitle: Three ingredients, "snickers" energy — the famous viral treat
image_prompt: Medjool dates filled with peanut butter and coated in dark chocolate, sprinkled with sea salt flakes on parchment

steps.title: Fill the dates
steps.text: Slit each date down one side, remove the pit and fill the cavity with peanut butter.

steps.title: Coat in chocolate
steps.text: Melt the dark chocolate and dip or drizzle each date until coated.

steps.title: Set & serve
steps.text: Sprinkle with sea-salt flakes and chill until the chocolate sets.
steps.tip: The salt is what makes them taste like a candy bar — don't skip it.

title: Dark Chocolate Almond & Cranberry Bark
subtitle: A 3-ingredient chocolate bark you make in ten minutes
image_prompt: Dark chocolate bark studded with whole almonds and dried cranberries, broken into shards on baking paper

steps.title: Melt the chocolate
steps.text: Melt the dark chocolate gently over a water bath or in short bursts in the microwave, stirring until smooth.

steps.title: Spread & top
steps.text: Spread the chocolate onto a lined tray and scatter over the toasted almonds, cranberries and a few sea-salt flakes.

steps.title: Set & break
steps.text: Chill until fully set, then break into rough pieces.

title: Roasted Honey Peaches with Yogurt
subtitle: Warm caramelised peaches over cool yogurt — summer in a bowl
image_prompt: Roasted peach halves caramelised with honey, served over Greek yogurt and topped with crushed pistachios, bright summer light

steps.title: Prep the peaches
steps.text: Halve and pit the peaches and place them cut-side up in a baking dish. Drizzle with the honey and a little cinnamon.

steps.title: Roast
steps.text: Roast at 200°C for about 20 minutes until soft and caramelised at the edges.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Spoon over the Greek yogurt, set the warm peaches on top and finish with crushed pistachios.

title: Classic Carrot Cake with Cream-Cheese Frosting
subtitle: The crowd-favourite bake — moist, spiced and generously frosted
image_prompt: Classic two-layer carrot cake with thick cream cheese frosting and chopped pecans on top, one slice cut to show the moist spiced crumb, soft natural light, editorial food photography

steps.title: Mix the batter
steps.text: Whisk the flour, baking soda, cinnamon and salt. In a second bowl whisk the oil, both sugars and vanilla, then beat in the eggs one at a time. Stir the dry mix into the wet in three parts, then fold in the carrots, pecans and raisins.
steps.tip: Don't overmix once the flour goes in — fold just until combined for a tender crumb.

steps.title: Bake
steps.text: Divide the batter between two lined 23 cm (9-inch) tins and bake at 180°C for 35–45 minutes, until the tops spring back when pressed. Cool completely before frosting.

steps.title: Make the frosting
steps.text: Beat the cream cheese until smooth, beat in the powdered sugar, then whip in the cold heavy cream until thick and spreadable.
steps.tip: Use cold cream and a block cream cheese (not spreadable tub) so the frosting holds its shape.

steps.title: Frost & serve
steps.text: Spread the frosting between and over the cooled layers, then finish with the remaining chopped pecans.

title: Strammer Max
subtitle: Ham and fried egg on buttered bread — ready in 5 minutes
image_prompt: Strammer Max — rye bread topped with ham and a sunny-side-up fried egg, chives, runny yolk, cosy German food photography

steps.title: Butter the bread
steps.text: Lightly toast the rye bread and spread with butter.

steps.title: Fry ham & eggs
steps.text: Warm the ham briefly in the pan, then fry the eggs sunny-side up.
steps.tip: Keep the yolks runny — they become the sauce.

steps.title: Build & serve
steps.text: Top the bread with ham and a fried egg, season and scatter with chives.

title: Pasta with Pesto
subtitle: Three ingredients, ten minutes — the ultimate easy dinner
image_prompt: Bowl of pasta tossed with green basil pesto, grated parmesan and cherry tomatoes, simple bright food photography

steps.title: Cook the pasta
steps.text: Boil the pasta in salted water until al dente, then drain — keep a splash of the cooking water.

steps.title: Toss with pesto
steps.text: Stir the pesto through the warm pasta, loosening with a little pasta water until glossy.
steps.tip: A splash of pasta water makes the pesto cling and turns it silky.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Top with grated parmesan and halved cherry tomatoes.

title: Pasta with Tomato Sauce
subtitle: The everyday classic — pantry to plate in 15 minutes
image_prompt: Simple bowl of pasta in tomato sauce topped with grated parmesan and basil, homely food photography

steps.title: Cook the pasta
steps.text: Boil the pasta in salted water until al dente, then drain.

steps.title: Quick tomato sauce
steps.text: Soften the garlic in a little olive oil, add the passata and oregano and simmer 5 minutes, then season.

steps.title: Combine & serve
steps.text: Toss the pasta in the sauce and top with grated parmesan.

title: Strawberry, Tomato & Burrata Salad
subtitle: Summer on a plate — juicy fruit, creamy burrata, herby vinaigrette
image_prompt: Strawberry, tomato and burrata salad on a platter with torn burrata, toasted pine nuts, fresh basil and a herby vinaigrette, bright summer food photography

steps.title: Make the vinaigrette
steps.text: Whisk the olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, finely chopped shallot, grated garlic, lemon zest and chopped herbs with salt and a pinch of chilli flakes.

steps.title: Marinate
steps.text: Halve the strawberries and toss with the cherry tomatoes and a third of the vinaigrette. Let sit for 10 minutes so the juices mingle.
steps.tip: Swap strawberries for peaches or cherries — if using peaches, sear the wedges cut-side down for 1–2 min first to deepen the flavour.

steps.title: Toast & assemble
steps.text: Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan until golden. Arrange the marinated fruit and tomatoes on a platter, tear the burrata over the top, drizzle with the remaining vinaigrette and scatter with pine nuts and extra herbs.

title: Spaghetti Bolognese
subtitle: The slow-simmered Italian classic the whole family asks for
image_prompt: Bowl of spaghetti bolognese with rich meat sauce, grated parmesan and basil, rustic Italian food photography

steps.title: Soffritto
steps.text: Finely chop the onion, carrot and garlic and soften gently in olive oil for about 8 minutes.
steps.tip: A slow, gentle soffritto is the flavour base — don't rush or brown it.

steps.title: Brown the mince
steps.text: Turn up the heat, add the beef mince and brown well, breaking it up with a spoon.

steps.title: Simmer the sauce
steps.text: Stir in the chopped tomatoes, tomato purée, oregano and bay leaf. Simmer on low for at least 30 minutes.

steps.title: Cook & toss
steps.text: Boil the spaghetti until al dente, drain, toss with the sauce and serve with grated parmesan.

title: Thai Green Chicken Curry
subtitle: Fragrant, creamy and on the table in 30 minutes
image_prompt: Thai green chicken curry with coconut sauce, green beans and red pepper over jasmine rice, fresh basil, vibrant food photography

steps.title: Cook the rice
steps.text: Rinse and cook the jasmine rice according to the packet.

steps.title: Fry the paste
steps.text: Fry the green curry paste in a little oil for 1 minute until fragrant.
steps.tip: Frying the paste first wakes up all the aromatics — don't skip it.

steps.title: Simmer the curry
steps.text: Add the sliced chicken and coconut milk with a splash of fish sauce and sugar. Simmer 10 minutes, then add the beans and pepper for 5 more.

steps.title: Finish
steps.text: Squeeze in the lime, scatter Thai basil and serve over the rice.

title: Margherita Pizza
subtitle: Blistered, bubbling and better than delivery
image_prompt: Classic margherita pizza with blistered crust, melted mozzarella, tomato and fresh basil, overhead shot, wood-fired look

steps.title: Heat the oven
steps.text: Heat the oven as hot as it goes (250°C) with a tray or stone inside.
steps.tip: A screaming-hot oven + preheated tray is the secret to a crisp base at home.

steps.title: Make the sauce
steps.text: Mix the passata with a little crushed garlic, oregano, olive oil and salt.

steps.title: Top the base
steps.text: Stretch the dough thin, spread a thin layer of sauce and tear over the mozzarella.

steps.title: Bake & finish
steps.text: Bake 8–12 minutes until blistered. Finish with fresh basil, parmesan and a drizzle of olive oil.

title: Classic Beef Lasagna
subtitle: Layers of rich ragù and creamy béchamel — the ultimate comfort bake
image_prompt: Classic beef lasagna with golden bubbling cheese top, one portion lifted showing layers of ragù and béchamel, cosy food photography

steps.title: Make the ragù
steps.text: Soften the onion and garlic, brown the mince, add the tomatoes and oregano and simmer 20 minutes.

steps.title: Make the béchamel
steps.text: Melt the butter, whisk in the flour, then gradually add the milk to make a smooth sauce. Season with a little nutmeg.

steps.title: Layer it up
steps.text: Layer ragù, lasagne sheets and béchamel, repeat, and finish with béchamel and parmesan.

steps.title: Bake
steps.text: Bake at 180°C for 35–40 minutes until golden and bubbling. Rest 10 minutes before cutting.
steps.tip: Resting before you cut lets the layers set so it slices cleanly.

title: Creamy Mac and Cheese
subtitle: Ultra-cheesy, crowd-pleasing comfort in one pan
image_prompt: Creamy macaroni and cheese with golden breadcrumb top in a baking dish, spoon pulling a cheesy portion, cosy food photography

steps.title: Cook the pasta
steps.text: Boil the macaroni until just al dente, then drain.

steps.title: Cheese sauce
steps.text: Melt the butter, whisk in the flour, add the milk gradually to a smooth sauce, then stir in the cheddar and a pinch of mustard powder.
steps.tip: Take the pan off the heat before adding the cheese so it stays silky, not grainy.

steps.title: Combine
steps.text: Fold the drained pasta into the cheese sauce and season.

steps.title: Bake (optional)
steps.text: Top with breadcrumbs and bake at 200°C for 15 minutes until golden, or serve straight away.

title: Chili con Carne
subtitle: Smoky, spiced and even better the next day
image_prompt: Bowl of chili con carne with kidney beans over rice, topped with coriander, warm rustic food photography

steps.title: Cook the rice
steps.text: Cook the rice according to the packet.

steps.title: Brown the base
steps.text: Soften the onion, garlic and pepper in oil, then add the mince and brown.

steps.title: Spice & simmer
steps.text: Stir in the cumin, paprika and chilli, then the tomatoes and beans. Simmer 30 minutes.
steps.tip: A long simmer melts everything together — the longer the better.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Season and serve over the rice.

title: Egg Fried Rice
subtitle: The 15-minute takeaway favourite, made at home
image_prompt: Egg fried rice with peas, carrot and spring onion in a wok, glossy and steaming, top-down food photography

steps.title: Scramble the eggs
steps.text: Beat and quickly scramble the eggs in a little oil, then set aside.

steps.title: Stir-fry the veg
steps.text: Stir-fry the garlic, carrot, peas and spring onions over high heat.
steps.tip: Use cold, day-old rice if you can — it fries up far better than fresh.

steps.title: Fry the rice
steps.text: Add the cooked rice and toss on high heat with a splash of soy sauce.

steps.title: Combine
steps.text: Return the eggs, drizzle with sesame oil, toss together and serve.

title: Marry Me Chicken
subtitle: The viral creamy sun-dried tomato chicken everyone is searching for
image_prompt: Marry me chicken — golden chicken breasts in creamy sun-dried tomato sauce with spinach and parmesan in a skillet, cosy food photography

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.text: Season the chicken breasts and sear in olive oil until golden on both sides, then set aside.

steps.title: Make the sauce
steps.text: Soften the garlic, then add the sun-dried tomatoes, cream, parmesan, oregano and a pinch of chilli.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.text: Return the chicken and simmer in the sauce for 10 minutes, then stir through the spinach.
steps.tip: Let it bubble gently so the sauce thickens and coats the chicken.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Serve with rice, pasta or crusty bread to mop up the sauce.

title: Chicken Fajita Bowl
subtitle: Charred peppers, spiced chicken and all the toppings
image_prompt: Chicken fajita bowl with charred peppers, black beans, rice, avocado and lime, colourful overhead food photography

steps.title: Cook the rice
steps.text: Cook the rice according to the packet.

steps.title: Season & cook chicken
steps.text: Toss the sliced chicken with cumin, paprika and salt, then cook until golden and done.

steps.title: Char the veg
steps.text: Cook the peppers and onion over high heat until charred and just soft.
steps.tip: Leave them undisturbed for a minute at a time to get that smoky char.

steps.title: Build the bowl
steps.text: Bowl up the rice and beans, add the chicken and veg, and top with avocado and a squeeze of lime.

title: Chicken Pad Thai
subtitle: Sweet, tangy, peanutty noodles — the Thai street-food icon
image_prompt: Chicken pad thai with rice noodles, beansprouts, crushed peanuts, spring onion and lime wedge, vibrant street-food photography

steps.title: Soak the noodles
steps.text: Soak the rice noodles in warm water until pliable, then drain.

steps.title: Cook chicken & egg
steps.text: Stir-fry the chicken until cooked, push to one side and scramble the eggs.

steps.title: Toss with sauce
steps.text: Add the noodles with the tamarind, fish sauce and sugar, and toss over high heat to coat.
steps.tip: Tamarind + fish sauce + sugar is the classic sweet-salty-sour balance — taste and adjust.

steps.title: Finish
steps.text: Add the beansprouts and spring onion, then top with crushed peanuts and a wedge of lime.

title: Best-Ever Vegetarian Lasagna
subtitle: Roasted veg and a rich tomato-béchamel — no one misses the meat
image_prompt: Vegetarian lasagna with roasted aubergine, courgette and pepper, golden mozzarella top, one slice lifted, cosy food photography

steps.title: Roast the veg
steps.text: Dice the aubergine, courgette and pepper, toss with olive oil and roast at 200°C for 20 minutes.
steps.tip: Roasting the veg first deepens the flavour and stops the lasagna going watery.

steps.title: Tomato sauce
steps.text: Simmer the chopped tomatoes with garlic and oregano for 15 minutes, then stir in the roasted veg.

steps.title: Béchamel
steps.text: Melt the butter, whisk in the flour, add the milk gradually to a smooth sauce and season with nutmeg.

steps.title: Layer & bake
steps.text: Layer veg sauce, lasagne sheets and béchamel, top with mozzarella and bake at 180°C for 35–40 minutes.

title: Fudgy Chocolate Brownies
subtitle: Crackly top, dense fudgy middle — the chocolate hit everyone wants
image_prompt: Stack of fudgy chocolate brownies with crackly tops and dense centres, chocolate chunks visible, moody food photography

steps.title: Melt
steps.text: Melt the dark chocolate with the butter until smooth, then let it cool slightly.

steps.title: Mix the batter
steps.text: Whisk the eggs, sugar and vanilla until thick and pale, then fold in the melted chocolate.

steps.title: Fold in dry
steps.text: Gently fold in the flour, cocoa and a pinch of salt — stop as soon as it's combined.
steps.tip: Under-mixing and under-baking slightly is the secret to a fudgy, not cakey, brownie.

steps.title: Bake & cut
steps.text: Pour into a lined tin and bake at 180°C for 20–25 minutes until just set. Cool fully, then cut into squares.
